methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $1.4B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Saronic
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series D), post-money $9.25B

private.name: Chaos Industries
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series D), post-money $4.5B

data.narrative:
Epirus, a Series D defense-technology company founded by Andy Lowery, is evaluated against a universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public and 8 private—operating in the Defense & Security sector at the same or adjacent stage. The peer set includes Saronic ($9.2B Series D), Flock Safety ($7.5B Series E), Chaos Industries ($4.5B Series D), Skydio ($2.2B Series E), and Hidden Level ($500M Series C).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier to the last post-money valuation of $1.4B, yielding $2.0B. Peer-median analysis across the five comparable companies produces a median valuation of $4.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $9.2B. The wide peer dispersion reflects stage heterogeneity and differentiated technology maturity within the sector.

Triangulation of both methods produces a valuation range of $1.7B to $5.2B, with a median of $3.3B. The current $1.35B post-money valuation sits below the low end of this range, suggesting potential upside in a next financing. Key uncertainties include revenue scale relative to peers, defensibility of intellectual property, and regulatory/export considerations inherent to defense-tech companies. The stage-progression method's modest result reflects conservative assumptions, while the peer-median result is sensitive to Saronic's outlier valuation and stage mixing within the comp set.
